consultation_boundary: Protocol E contradiction-first consults only at decision gates after baseline convergence.
final_answer: The primary failure mechanism was a socio-technical design-assurance failure: Boeing and FAA processes allowed an unsafe MCAS control-law implementation, invalid pilot-response assumptions, weak disclosure/training boundaries, and derivative-program pressures to align without adequate independent barriers.

primary_metric.definition: Number of viable explanations still active immediately before final convergence.
primary_metric.final_convergence_rule: Converge only after preserving all six explanations with roles: primary, proximate, latent, human-factors, trigger, and architecture.

rankings.rationale: Best explains how the unsafe MCAS behavior, pilot-response assumptions, documentation/training decisions, and FAA/Boeing review gaps passed through the development path.
The lethal technical expression was MCAS using erroneous AOA to command repeated stabilizer nose-down trim.
Invalid assumptions about recognition and response are part of the design-assurance failure, not post-hoc blame assignment.
Program pressure and training-continuity incentives plausibly shaped the disclosure, training, and derivative-design path.

rankings.hypothesis: AOA sensor, maintenance, and accident-specific trigger chain
rankings.rationale: Erroneous AOA was necessary to both accident sequences, but the AOA details do not explain why one failure could become catastrophic.

rankings.hypothesis: Derivative-airframe architecture and changed-product legacy constraints
rankings.rationale: The derivative architecture and changed-product frame shaped the need for MCAS and the limits of alerting/training changes.
